name: Lee's Art Shop
text: My wife is an artist, so sometimes I pick up art supplies for her. I went to Lee's to purchase some markers, but the clueless staff directed me to the wrong ones. Not really knowing the difference, I purchased them, only to fins out when I got home they were the wrong kind. I tried to return them the next day, explaining my mistake, but the staff just stonewalled me saying they do not accept marker returns - they even insinuated that I had taken them home, used them, and was trying to return them now that I was done. From now on, I'm going to shop online, or go downtown to Blick's or AI Friedman, or someplace that's actually willing to work with a customer to resolve a dispute.

name: Icon Parking
text: We arrived and asked how much it would be to park and leave by Saturday morning at 10:00 am. We asked this clear as day, several times, and confirmed several times. Jose told us very plainly that to leave at that time on Saturday would be $82. When we arrived at that time on Saturday, he told us the price was now $164, and that we had misunderstood him. We hadn't. We asked to speak to his boss and insisted that he was the boss, that we had misunderstood, and that we had to pay $164. Just be aware this place will mislead you and ream you if it gets a chance.

text: Placed my Zx14r for winter storage and transfered to full membership, absolutely amazing facilities, the team here is amazing, friendly, warm and welcoming. They treat you like family and you gain not just new friends but tons of benefits you wouldn't from any other place

text: I can’t say enough good things about this garage. Highly secure, great owners, excellent service. It’s the most gorgeous space and the prices are affordable. Highly recommend.

name: Gotham Depot Moto
text: Love this garage. The Owners are great and provide a great place to garage your bike. The garage is maintained very well and love that I can work on my bike here. I am working remote so the membership is works for me. They have a really awesome lounge/work area with drinks, seating and awesome TV. I have been able to get all my work done here. Really happy I found this place. It is literally everything I need all in one.

name: Gotham Depot Moto
text: The owners, the garage, the neighborhood, all tremendous... You won't find a better motorcycle garage in NYC. The facility is highly secured with electronic key cards and facial recognition software. I highly recommend you come see the garage for yourself.

name: Gotham Depot Moto
text: By far and away the best experience I've ever had storing my bike. Beautiful space with a great mezzanine lounge area; safe and secure spot with a wonderful group of owners and patrons. This is exactly what the NYC riding community needs!

name: Gotham Depot Moto
text: Ronnie and Stefon are the absolute best! It was completely fate that the coolest and most secure garage/club in the city would open up down the street from me. The amenities are second to none and the owners are some of the most hospitable people I have ever met. This is something you need to experience for yourself in order to truly appreciate it. I plan to be here for a long long time after having my last bike stolen off the street within six months of purchasing it. If you have the opportunity to store your bike here, don’t think twice. There is nowhere else like this place.

name: Gotham Depot Moto
text: Met the owners Ronnie and Stephan, great guys, when they rescued me stranded on the B.Q.E. Their garage is 24/7 with great security (facial recognition) in a great neighborhood, spacious with each bike having ample space for maintenance, water for washing, personal locker space, personal outlet (battery tender), kitchenette and large mezzanine sky lounge (for that after ride cocktail). In all a beautiful, safe and comfortable space.

name: Gotham Depot Moto
text: one of the first members here and I have to say it is worth every penny compare to the other garages in the area and I’ve seen most of them. Not only is the space safe for your bike but the amenities are like no other and of course Stefon and Ronnie are awesome. So friendly and welcoming, going above and beyond to make you feel right at home. It’s not just a space for your bike, it’s a place to hangout, relax and make new friends :) GDM is a true reflection of the NYC bike community: one big family.

name: Manhattan Community Boathouse - Pier 96
text: I first came here back in 2016 for the free kayaking. It was a blazing hot day and I just HAD to find a way to get out on the water, somewhere in the city. Manhattan Community Boathouse was just the place! I felt so welcomed and had so much fun during my first visit that I decided to become a volunteer. 5 years later, I still spend almost every summer weekend here, helping the organization offer the public the priceless (FREE!) experience of paddling on the Hudson river. Sharing this unique experience with the public is one of my favorite things to do in the city, and seeing the smiles on people's faces makes it all worth it. As a volunteer, I enjoy the extra training classes offered and volunteer-only kayaking trips we get to go on as well! A few things to know for the 2021 season, some of which is different from prior year reviews: MCBH is operating by reservation to prevent overcrowding. They are open Saturdays and Sundays from 12 PM - 6 PM through October 10. You can reserve your spot up to 2 weeks in advance on the website. Paddlers of all skill levels are invited to participate as long as you know how to swim. Anyone under the age of 18 must be accompanied by a parent or guardian. They offer changing rooms and lockers with locks provided, so you can bring a change of clothes if you want, because you will get wet. There's no public restroom here though, so be sure to stop in Clinton Dewitt park at 54th & 11th Ave before walking over if you need to use the facilities! Otherwise, a portapotty along the bike path near the boathouse is the only toilet option. Did I mention it's all free? The program is run entirely by volunteers, and funded solely by donations from the public, but there is absolutely no pressure to donate. Whether you come as a volunteer or as a member of the public, I think this is the best free activity in NYC!

name: Manhattan Community Boathouse - Pier 96
text: The staff are very helpful. The experience was awesome. You definitely don’t want to miss it!

text: Love the experience the staff is wonderful my family had a wonderful experience

I have paddled here before and in the river but it has always been fun, go with the flow, Enjoy ! ( not sure if Manhattan Kayak Co was located here before with Eric )
